answer:
**The work is *Kuñjarakarṇa Dharmakathana* (unknown author), Chapter 1.**[^1]

The ascetic Kuñjarakarṇa, sent by the Buddha on a tour of hellish realms, arrives at Yama's palace and sees a great copper cauldron (previously described as "a great copper cauldron with many spouts") being washed clean in the courtyard.[^2] He asks Yama about it:

> \"Lord Yama, may your glory be eternal.  
> Your compassion for this Kuñjara is fruitful.  
> But what is that cauldron that is being washed?  
> What is its purpose, now that it is clean?\"[^3]  
>   
> \"I am puzzled by it, for it is to be filled,  
> yet it is turned upside down, with nothing inside.  
> What is the reason for it being so empty?  
> This is my question; please tell me.\"[^4]

Yama replies that a great sinner (revealed later as the gandharva king Pūrṇavijaya) is expected soon, with his punishment beginning in the cauldron:

> \"There is a great sinner who is to be cast in.  
> His time in torment will be long.  
> For a hundred thousand years is his term;  
> when it is full, he will be in the cauldron of misery.\"[^5]  
>   
> \"After that, the weapon-birds will come  
> to tear apart that wicked, defiled soul.  
> It will be displayed, they say, in the iron tree,  
> and dashed against the sharp sword-blades.\"[^6]  
>   
> \"When that is done, it will be brought to the iron plain,  
> and hung by its feet from a bamboo pole.  
> A fire will burn beneath it without cease.  
> Its time in each torment is the same.\"[^7]  
>   
> \"But as for his arrival in Yama's realm,  
> in seven nights he is expected to come.  
> That is the reason the cauldron is being washed,  
> as the place of his torment when he arrives.\"[^8]

[^1]: *Kuñjarakarṇa Dharmakathana*, Chapter 1 ([/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/](#p-693983)).

[^2]: *Kuñjarakarṇa Dharmakathana*, Chapter 1 ([/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-693983](#p-693983)).

[^3]: *Kuñjarakarṇa Dharmakathana*, Chapter 1 ([/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-c32761](#p-c32761)).

[^4]: *Kuñjarakarṇa Dharmakathana*, Chapter 1 ([/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-2efb11](#p-2efb11)).

[^5]: *Kuñjarakarṇa Dharmakathana*, Chapter 1 ([/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-f2b796](#p-f2b796)).

[^6]: *Kuñjarakarṇa Dharmakathana*, Chapter 1 ([/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-6729cc](#p-6729cc)).

[^7]: *Kuñjarakarṇa Dharmakathana*, Chapter 1 ([/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-890b15](#p-890b15)).

"hundred thousand" OR "hundred-thousand" cauldron OR hell OR death
